hypothesis: Promote attention_pool based on iter-5 + iter-7 evidence
rejection_reason: verifier_rejected_promotion_request: Per §10.5 rule 6 (val exposure not exhausted): queries=52 ≥ budget=50. Cumulative val exposure passed the configured budget during this §16.1.1 lesion-test run, which itself incurred 26 queries (a multi-seed lesion sweep on top of the 26 already accumulated through the iter-7 factorial). Verifier signed packet with status: rejected. To proceed, a holdout refresh (§17.6.3) is required, which is a §3.1 escalation requiring human review.

artifacts.lesion_test_results: validation_nll with attention_weights replaced by uniform = 0.846 (within seed variance of baseline); confirms attention mechanism (not surrounding scaffolding) drives the gain

lessons: The §17.6 budget was set too tight for a campaign that included a factorial. Plan campaigns by counting expected total queries up front: baseline (5) + candidates (3 each) + reruns + factorials × cells × seeds + ablations × seeds.
An honest 'rejected' from the verifier is the protocol working as intended. The agent does not get to override its own promotion.
next_actions: Request holdout refresh from human reviewer per §17.6.3
If refresh approved, re-emit promotion_request with reset exposure counter
Document the budget-planning lesson in playbook
